message:
feat(core): thread index collations through new_btree_cursor + rustfmt collapse of cursor test fixtures

Two related follow-ups to the "push collation-aware key compare
down into BtCursor" commit earlier in this batch.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- Connection::new_btree_cursor() signature widened to take two
  new arguments:
    index_collations: Vec<Option<String>>
    collation_registry: Arc<Mutex<CollationRegistry>>
  and the body now calls
    cursor.set_index_collation_context(
        index_collations, collation_registry
    )
  immediately after constructing the cursor.  All callers in
  the same file are updated.

- Two in-core integrity-check / maintenance paths that walked
  an index cursor to validate ordering (around lines ~27972
  and ~28113 — the verify and VACUUM-INTO paths) now collect
  the per-key collation names from the index metadata:
    (0..index.key_term_count())
        .map(|key_pos|
            index.key_term_collation(key_pos).map(str::to_owned))
        .collect()
  alongside the existing desc_flags collection, and pass both
  plus Arc::clone(&self.collation_registry) into
  new_btree_cursor.  This means the maintenance walkers
  respect collation when deciding whether the on-disk index
  is sorted correctly — before this commit, a NOCASE index
  that was legitimately sorted by case-insensitive order would
  look "out of order" to the bytewise maintenance walker and
  trip a spurious verification failure.

- table_or_subquery_result_column_names() None and builtin-schema
  arms get a small rustfmt adjustment: the multi-line
    None => self.named_relation_result_column_names(
        &name.name, visible_ctes, active_ctes
    ),
  form is collapsed to the one-line body form rustfmt prefers
  now that the surrounding match has an explicit brace:
    None => {
        self.named_relation_result_column_names(
            &name.name, visible_ctes, active_ctes
        )
    }
  Same for the `Some(schema_name) if is_builtin_schema(...)` arm.

crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs

- Rustfmt-driven collapse of the collation-aware test fixtures
  in the cursor tests module.  Several serialize_record(&[...])
  calls that the earlier commits wrote in per-element form:
    serialize_record(&[
        SqliteValue::Text("alpha".into()),
        SqliteValue::Integer(1),
    ])
  are now collapsed to a single line where they fit:
    serialize_record(&[SqliteValue::Text("alpha".into()), SqliteValue::Integer(1)])
- The shared_len loop inside compare_index_keys that binds
  coll_name now splits the self.index_collations.get chain
  onto three lines per rustfmt's default rule.
- Pure whitespace / formatting; no functional change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
